text: ​​ @radaf4429  art is subjective. This is magnificent for people who have a silly sense of humor, but annoying to those who don't. If I want fine art, I'll go to a museum. But I feel like laughing at shit posts, so today I'm here.

text: @watchrosie  The appreciation and interpretation of art can be subjective, but art itself has a rather concrete definition.
author: @radaf4429

text: ​ @radaf4429  I just checked the definition of art (the expression or application of human creative skill and imagination, typically in a visual form such as painting or sculpture, producing works to be appreciated primarily for their beauty or emotional power) so yeah YTPS 100% counts as art, since it's a clear expression of creative skill and imagination to make the stuff he makes. The sentence mixing especially is amazing.
author: @thunderspark1536

text: @thunderspark1536  I never called that into question.
author: @radaf4429

text:
@radaf4429  But you did state that something being called art isn't a high bar, which I'd argue it is. It's difficult to make something truly yourself, to be creative and put that content out for the world to see.

Cs188 has stayed consistent with his artistic vision for over a decade, and that's worthy of applause.
author: @thunderspark1536

text:
@thunderspark1536  No, something being art doesn't say anything about its integrity. Not necessarily. There are plenty of people who spend years making purposefully bad art. There are people who create amazing art with minimal effort. "Art" is ultimately the frame we put around something that we want others to see.

Things that can be considered art:

A random picture you take with your phone
A signature, or even just a scribble
A grilled cheese sandwich
Nothing at all

The idea that art is linked to beauty or the essence of some other qualifier was deconstructed at least 100 years ago with the futurism movement and Dada.
author: @radaf4429

text: Fun fact- during WW2, Allied armored vehicle fuel lines were cracking from the extreme cold.
